settings.notes: v0.6.0 Evidence Fusion: text evidence + context + behavior chain + intent evidence + strict red gate + finding navigation. Report only; not a malware verdict.

rules.recommendation: Review whether a command or payload is being hidden. Do not run until the context is clear.
rules.whyFlagged: Encoded or base64 command execution can hide what will actually run.
rules.falsePositiveNote: This can be legitimate in admin scripts. A single signal is not proof of malware.

rules.recommendation: Review any dynamic code execution, especially when input comes from network/files.
rules.whyFlagged: Dynamic execution runs text as code, which increases review difficulty.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Some tools use this legitimately. Review source and inputs before judging.

rules.recommendation: Review the URL, downloaded file, and hash before running anything.
rules.whyFlagged: Download-then-run behavior is used by both installers and malicious payloads.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Installers often download files. This is a review signal, not an accusation.

rules.recommendation: Review whether this Windows system tool is expected in this project.
rules.whyFlagged: Built-in Windows tools can be used for admin work or abuse.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Common in IT/admin scripts. Context matters.

rules.recommendation: Do not run until the security-setting change is verified as intentional.
rules.whyFlagged: Changing Defender settings may reduce endpoint protection.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Some controlled admin tools modify exclusions. Verify before acting.

rules.recommendation: Review the target path and backup status before running.
rules.whyFlagged: Recursive or forced delete commands can cause data loss.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Cleanup scripts can be legitimate. Confirm path and scope.

rules.recommendation: Review whether permission changes are necessary and scoped.
rules.whyFlagged: Permission changes can be legitimate but risky if broad.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Normal in deployment/admin scripts. Confirm target path.

rules.recommendation: Read the command on the right side before npm install.
rules.whyFlagged: Package lifecycle scripts can run automatically during installation.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Many packages use lifecycle scripts for legitimate build steps.

rules.recommendation: Review what OS command can be executed and where input comes from.
rules.whyFlagged: Node code can launch OS commands.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Build tools often spawn commands. Review before judging.

rules.whyFlagged: Python code can run shell commands or dynamic code.

rules.recommendation: Review run commands, external actions, secrets, and permissions.
rules.whyFlagged: CI workflows can run commands and access deployment secrets.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Workflows are normal DevOps files. This is informational.

rules.recommendation: Review editor tasks before opening unknown repositories.
rules.whyFlagged: Editor tasks can run commands in the workspace.
rules.falsePositiveNote: VS Code tasks are normal in many projects.

rules.recommendation: Review agent tools, external commands, and instruction files.
rules.whyFlagged: AI-agent config can define tools, prompts, or commands an agent may follow.
rules.falsePositiveNote: Agent config can be normal. Review scope and permissions.

rules.recommendation: If this is a real key, remove it from the repo and rotate it.
rules.whyFlagged: This may indicate an OpenAI API key or related environment variable.
rules.falsePositiveNote: May be only a variable name or placeholder. Verify the value.

rules.recommendation: If this is a real token, revoke/rotate it and remove it from history if published.
rules.whyFlagged: This may indicate a GitHub token or token variable.
rules.falsePositiveNote: May be only a variable name or placeholder. Verify the value.

rules.recommendation: If this is a real token, revoke/rotate it.
rules.whyFlagged: This may indicate an npm token or token variable.
rules.falsePositiveNote: May be only a variable name or placeholder.

rules.recommendation: If real, rotate the secret and move it to a secret manager or uncommitted .env file.
rules.whyFlagged: This may indicate a cloud/database/private key exposure.
rules.falsePositiveNote: May be a placeholder or empty variable. Verify before judging.

rules.recommendation: Check whether this is a real secret or only sample/test data.
